text: Hello, this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 大家好,这里是BBC英语学习的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Pippa. In this episode, we're discussing poetry.
zh: 我是Neil。我是Pippa。这一期我们要讨论诗歌。

text: For some, poetry is what you study at school, then forget, while for others it becomes an important part of their life.
zh: 对有些人来说,诗歌是学生时代学过、之后就忘掉的东西,但对另一些人来说,它却成为生活中重要的一部分。

text: Have you ever read or written any poetry, Neil? Well, I haven't written any poetry since I was at school.
zh: Neil,你读过或写过诗吗?我从学生时代之后就再没写过诗了。

text: I would love to read more poetry, but I just don't get it really.
zh: 我很想多读一些诗,但说实话我看不太懂。

text: I find it difficult to understand. A good place to hear poetry is an open mic night,
zh: 我觉得理解起来很困难。要听诗歌,一个好去处就是开放麦之夜,

text: a live event where anyone is allowed to stand up on stage to sing or recite poems. Here,
zh: 这是一种现场活动,任何人都可以上台演唱或朗诵诗歌。接下来,

text: BBC reporter Maria recites her open mic poem about a friend who suffered a stroke for BBC World Service programme People Fixing the World.
zh: BBC记者Maria在开放麦上朗诵了她为BBC世界服务节目《解决世界难题的人们》创作的一首诗,讲述了一位中风朋友的故事。

text: This poem, I wrote it about a friend who I love dearly.
zh: 这首诗,是我为一位我深爱的朋友写的。

text: It's called Stroke. You're awake and trying to grasp who it is.
zh: 它叫《中风》。你醒来,想弄清楚眼前的人是谁。

text: I pull my mask past my lips and you study my face. Eyes wide.
zh: 我把口罩拉过嘴唇,你端详着我的脸。眼睛睁得很大。

text: I've enough stuff to tell you, I say, sinking into a chair.
zh: 我说,我有很多事想告诉你,一边说一边坐进椅子里。

text: I stare. Sometimes if you go to an open mic night,
zh: 我凝视着。有时候,如果你去参加开放麦之夜,

text: people can really touch your soul with what they're saying and even if you're having a bad day, life can feel so much better after going to an open mic night.
zh: 人们说出的话真的能触动你的灵魂,即使你那天过得很糟糕,去过开放麦之夜后,生活也会感觉好很多。

text: For Maria, poetry has the power to heal emotions and touch your soul,
zh: 对Maria来说,诗歌有治愈情感、触动灵魂的力量,

text: meaning to affect you deeply on an emotional or spiritual level.
zh: 意思是从情感或精神层面深深地打动你。

text: In this episode, we'll hear more about the positive power of poetry and as usual, we'll learn some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 在这一期节目中,我们将进一步了解诗歌的积极力量,像往常一样,我们也会学习一些实用的新词汇和短语。

text: And Remember, you'll find all the vocabulary, plus a quiz and worksheet, on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记住,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到所有词汇,以及一个测验和练习题。

text: But first, I have a question for you, Pippa. Just now you used the phrase,
zh: 不过首先,Pippa,我有个问题要问你。你刚才用了一个短语,

text: the positive power of poetry, repeating the same initial p sound across a string of words.
zh: “诗歌的积极力量”,连续几个词都以相同的辅音p开头。

text: Doing this is a common feature of poetry, but what's it called?
zh: 这样做是诗歌中常见的一种手法,但它叫什么呢?

text: Is it a assonance, b alliteration, or c simile?
zh: 是a. 谐音,b. 头韵,还是c. 明喻?

text: Finally, my English degree might come in handy and I'm going to say b alliteration.
zh: 看来我的英语学位终于派上用场了,我要说是b,头韵。

text: Well, we'll find out at the end and I hope you don't get embarrassed, Pippa.
zh: 我们最后会揭晓答案,希望你别太尴尬,Pippa。

text: Now, poetry helped Maria overcome sadness.
zh: 诗歌帮助Maria战胜了悲伤。

text: But could its power to heal work in other areas too?
zh: 但它的治愈力量能否在其他领域也发挥作用呢?

text: Hot Poets is a group helping people understand the science behind global issues like climate change.
zh: Hot Poets是一个帮助人们理解全球性问题(如气候变化)背后科学原理的组织。

text: They go to meetings like the UN Climate Conference and listen to scientific papers being presented.
zh: 他们参加像联合国气候大会这样的会议,聆听科学论文的发表。

text: Then they write a poem about it all and perform that back to the audience.
zh: 然后他们把这一切写成诗,再朗诵给听众听。

text: Here's hot poet Liv Talk reciting her poem to BBC World Service's People Fixing the World.
zh: 下面是Hot Poets诗人Liv Talk为BBC世界服务节目《解决世界难题的人们》朗诵的诗。

text: It starts with an adventure at sea. A big blue murder mystery where the killer is not the whale,
zh: 故事从一场海上冒险开始。这是一桩巨大的蓝色谋杀悬案,凶手不是鲸鱼,

text: but an epic humanity fail of overfishing and polluting at unimaginable scale.
zh: 而是人类在过度捕捞和污染方面达到难以想象规模的一次史诗级失败。

text: Liv's poem is based on a study into biodiversity.
zh: Liv的这首诗是根据一项关于生物多样性的研究创作的。

text: It's a complex research project that many find hard to understand.
zh: 这是一个复杂的研究项目,很多人都难以理解。

text: Here, Liv discusses this with Myra Anubi for BBC World Service programme, People Fixing the World.
zh: 在这里,Liv与Myra Anubi一起,为BBC世界服务节目《解决世界难题的人们》讨论了这个话题。

text: And Liv, I mean, so what you've identified is that there is an issue around the communication of science.
zh: Liv,你发现的问题是,科学传播方面存在障碍,是这样吗?

text: Yes, a lot of this jargon is really hard to understand or a lot of the science is quite impenetrable to normal people, so it's instantly a turn -off.
zh: 是的,很多专业术语真的很难理解,很多科学内容对普通人来说相当难以理解,所以一下子就让人失去兴趣了。

text: Science uses lots of jargon – specialist or technical vocabulary, which is quite different from everyday language.
zh: 科学使用大量行话——也就是专业或技术性词汇,这与日常语言有很大不同。

text: For example, a scientist might use the word precipitation, while in everyday English we would say rain.
zh: 比如,科学家可能会用“降水”这个词,而在日常英语中我们会说“下雨”。

text: Jargon can make scientific papers impenetrable – an adjective meaning impossible to understand.
zh: 行话会让科学论文变得难以理解——impenetrable这个形容词的意思就是无法理解的。

text: And worse, it makes science a turn -off – something that people dislike or find uninteresting.
zh: 更糟的是,它让科学变得令人却步——也就是让人不喜欢或觉得没意思的东西。

text: Hot poets try to change all of that by making complex environmental ideas easy to understand through poetry.
zh: Hot Poets试图通过诗歌,把复杂的环保理念变得通俗易懂,以此改变这一切。

text: And it works, says Cat Bruce, the environmental scientist whose study Liv based her poem on,
zh: 环境科学家Cat Bruce说,这方法确实有效,Liv的诗正是根据她的研究创作的,

text: talking here with Myra Anubi for BBC World Service's People Fixing the World.
zh: 她在这里与Myra Anubi一起,为BBC世界服务节目《解决世界难题的人们》进行了交谈。

text: What did it mean for you, Cat, hearing the poem, having actually experienced everything she's saying?
zh: Cat,当你亲身经历过她所讲述的一切后,再听到这首诗,对你来说意味着什么?

text: It still gives me goosebumps when I hear it now. now.
zh: 直到现在,每次听到它我还是会起鸡皮疙瘩。

text: I think it's everything that I wish I could say and put into words myself.
zh: 我觉得,这正是我一直希望自己能说出来、写出来的东西。

text: I feel like Liv just, you know, has done that. Liv's poem gave Kat goosebumps,
zh: 我感觉Liv把它表达出来了。Liv的诗让Kat起了鸡皮疙瘩,

text: meaning she was so emotionally affected that the hairs on her skin stood up, making tiny bumps.
zh: 意思是她被深深地打动了,以至于皮肤上的汗毛都竖了起来,形成了小疙瘩。

text: Maybe poetry can't fix everything, but for Kat, it's making an impact for good.
zh: 也许诗歌不能解决所有问题,但对Kat来说,它正在产生积极的影响。

text: Now, what was the answer to your question, Neil? Well, you seemed very clear,
zh: Neil,你那个问题的答案是什么?你当时看起来很确定,

text: Pippa, that when you use a string of words beginning with the same sound, it's called alliteration.
zh: Pippa,你说当一连串单词以相同的音开头时,这叫做头韵。

text: And you're absolutely right. Phew. OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt,
zh: 你完全说对了。呼,好的,让我们回顾一下今天学到的词汇,

text: starting with open mic night, a live event where anyone is allowed to perform poems or music on stage.
zh: 首先是open mic night(开放麦之夜),指任何人都可以上台表演诗歌或音乐的现场活动。

text: If something touches your soul, it affects you deeply on an emotional or spiritual level.
zh: 如果某样东西touches your soul(触动你的灵魂),意味着它从情感或精神层面深深地打动了你。

text: Jargon means special words and phrases that are used in a particular profession or subject.
zh: jargon(行话)指某个行业或学科中使用的专门词汇和短语。

text: The adjective impenetrable means impossible to understand.
zh: 形容词impenetrable(难以理解的)意思是无法理解的。

text: A turn -off is something that people dislike or find uninteresting.
zh: turn-off指人们不喜欢或觉得没意思的东西。

text: And finally, if you get goosebumps, the hairs on your skin stand up because you're cold, emotionally affected or afraid.
zh: 最后,如果你get goosebumps(起鸡皮疙瘩),意味着你的皮肤上汗毛竖起,原因可能是寒冷、情感触动或害怕。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up,
zh: 我们的六分钟又一次结束了,

text: but remember you'll find lots more episodes as well as a quiz and worksheet for this one on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 不过别忘了,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到更多期节目,以及本期的测验和练习题。

text: See you again soon, but for now, it's Goodbye.
zh: 我们很快再见,现在,先说再见。
